description: Structured AST configuration matrix for the convention gate (C-304 Tier 1). Each rule maps to a deterministic check that runs without LLM invocation.

rules.name: File Path Comment
rules.description: Every source file must include its relative path from the monorepo root as a comment on line 1.

rules.name: Snake Case File Names
rules.description: All source files must use snake_case naming. Enforced by Biome useFilenamingConvention.

rules.name: Route Boundary — No Server Routes
rules.description: The client is a static SPA. +server.ts, +page.server.ts, and +layout.server.ts files must NOT exist under apps/frontend/client/src/routes/.

patterns.message: 🔴 SPA boundary violation: +server.ts files are forbidden in client routes
🔴 SPA boundary violation: +page.server.ts files are forbidden in client routes
🔴 SPA boundary violation: +layout.server.ts files are forbidden in client routes

rules.name: Options Object Signature
rules.description: Functions with more than 1 argument must use an options object pattern. Exception: class constructors and method overrides.

patterns.message: 🔴 Function has >1 positional argument — must use options object

rules.name: Private Field Underscore Prefix
rules.description: All private class members must use a leading underscore (_) prefix. Exception: $state ViewModel fields.

patterns.message: 🔴 Private member lacks _ prefix — required by aikami-conventions

rules.name: Import from Package Root
rules.description: Never import from lib/ sub-paths of shared packages. Always import from the package root (maps to src/index.ts).

patterns.message: 🔴 Import from lib/ sub-path — use package root import instead

rules.name: Logger Alias Enforcement
rules.description: Always import logger from $logger alias, never from @aikami/logger directly.

patterns.message: 🔴 Import from @aikami/logger — use $logger alias instead

rules.name: Type Import — No Inline import()
rules.description: Type references must use top-level import type statements, never inline import() expressions.

patterns.message: 🔴 Inline import() type — use top-level import type instead

rules.name: ViewModel Separation
rules.description: Svelte views (.svelte) must not contain data transformation, logic, or state. All logic belongs in the ViewModel.

patterns.message: ⚠️ View contains $state/$derived/$effect — move to ViewModel
⚠️ View imports from $lib/services — delegate to ViewModel

rules.name: ViewModel Factory Export
rules.description: ViewModels must be exported via a factory function (getXxxViewModel), never as raw classes.

patterns.message: ⚠️ ViewModel exported as raw class — use factory function pattern

rules.name: No any Type
rules.description: Never use the any type. Use unknown with type guards instead. Enforced by Biome noExplicitAny.

rules.name: No null Usage
rules.description: Use undefined instead of null everywhere in the codebase.

patterns.message: ⚠️ null usage — prefer undefined

rules.name: No Type/Schema in Service Files
rules.description: Service files must not export data, types, or schemas. These belong in dedicated packages.

patterns.message: 🔴 Data/type/schema exported from service file — move to shared package

rules.name: No Server Routes in SPA
rules.description: The SvelteKit client must not contain server-side routes or endpoints. All data fetching is client-side.

patterns.message: 🔴 Server endpoint in SPA — use client-side data fetching

rules.name: No Raw Dictionaries in ViewModels
rules.description: ViewModels must not declare raw translation dictionaries or constant lookup tables. These belong in packages/shared/constants/.

patterns.message: 🔴 Raw label dictionary in ViewModel — extract to packages/shared/constants/
🔴 Static label dictionary in ViewModel — extract to packages/shared/constants/

rules.name: No TypeBox Outside Schemas Package
rules.description: TypeBox imports and Type.* declarations are forbidden outside packages/shared/schemas/ and test files. Schemas are the single source of truth — they live in one place only.

patterns.message: 🔴 TypeBox schema outside packages/shared/schemas/ — move schema to shared package
🔴 TypeBox schema in ViewModel — move to packages/shared/schemas/
🔴 TypeBox schema in Svelte view — schemas belong in packages/shared/schemas/
🔴 @sinclair/typebox import outside schemas/types package — schemas belong in packages/shared/schemas/

rules.name: No PixiJS Import in ViewModels
rules.description: ViewModels must never import pixi.js or hook into the game ticker. Use EngineBridge for all game communication.

patterns.message: 🔴 pixi.js import in ViewModel — use EngineBridge, never app.ticker directly
🔴 pixi.js import in Svelte view — game rendering belongs in engine package
🔴 app.ticker.add() in ViewModel — ticker ownership belongs to packages/frontend/engine/

rules.name: No Duplicate Types from TypeBox Schemas
rules.description: Types in packages/shared/types/ must derive from TypeBox schemas via Static<typeof Schema>. Never duplicate interfaces manually.

patterns.message: ⚠️ Raw type definition in shared/types — derive from TypeBox schema via Static<typeof Schema> instead

rules.name: No Abbreviations in Shared Packages
rules.description: Shared packages must use fully-spelled identifiers, never abbreviations. Exceptions: well-known acronyms (URL, API, ID, HTML, CSS, JSON, HTTP).

patterns.message: ⚠️ Abbreviation in shared package — use full word (context not ctx, options not opts, function not fn, config not cfg, etc.)

tier2.description: LLM Cognitive Review Pass — only invoked after Tier 1 passes.

checks.name: View State Audit
checks.prompt: Audit Svelte views for logic violations: state definitions, derived computations, data transformations, and service imports inside .svelte files.
checks.failOn: Any view contains $state, $derived, data transformation, or service imports

checks.name: ViewModel Boundary Audit
checks.prompt: Audit ViewModels for boundary violations: direct database references, repository imports, or API calls that should be delegated to services.
checks.failOn: ViewModel imports from repositories, database layers, or makes direct API/firebase calls

checks.name: AST Structure Skeleton Review
checks.prompt: Review the provided AST-outline skeleton + unified patch delta for logical violations. Only type declarations and structural outlines are provided.
checks.failOn: Structural regression, removed security guards, or convention violations visible in type skeleton
